mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not exceed 55% during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of stable inflation indicate healthy capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns when inflation exceeds 0.5% monthly, or INDPRO growth correlates negatively with SPX in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects continuous economic expansion that reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or payroll growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX direction.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with declining real GDP growth signal accommodative policy offsetting economic weakness, which supports equity markets over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Combined low-rate and low-growth environment produces negative 60-day returns in over 55% of observations, invalidating the policy offset mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment that drags on related corporate revenues and triggers negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or housing declines show no predictive power for SPX direction.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during periods of rising unemployment indicates a temporary inventory restocking cycle that fails to sustain earnings and leads to short-term equity corrections.
failure_condition: Signal yields positive 5-day returns in over 50% of triggered cases, or the divergence pattern does not predict SPX direction in walk-forward tests.
